<!-- Generated By: Claude Code (Claude Opus 4.7) -->

# Helm Silently Ignores Unknown Values Keys

Helm has no schema enforcement by default. A misspelled or mis-routed override
in `values.yaml` produces no warning and no error — the chart renders with
defaults and the override has no effect.

> The prescriptive rule (verify every new override with `helm template` before
> pushing) and command shape live in
> [standards/kubernetes/helm-values.md](https://github.com/jewzaam/standards/blob/main/kubernetes/helm-values.md).

## Why this matters

- `values.schema.json` is optional and most charts ship without one.
- Umbrella charts (e.g. kube-prometheus-stack) namespace subchart values
  under the subchart name (`grafana.*`, `prometheus.*`). The subchart's own
  value keys still apply — the umbrella does not rename them.
- Subchart key names drift between major versions. An override that worked
  in v11 of a subchart may be silently ignored in v12.
- GitOps controllers (Argo CD, Flux) reconcile happily on a misspelled
  override — the live cluster matches the rendered chart, the controller
  reports "Synced", and the bug is invisible until something downstream
  fails to behave.

## Concrete failure mode

The grafana subchart at version 12.x uses `annotations` (top-level
Deployment annotations), **not** `deploymentAnnotations`. Setting
`grafana.deploymentAnnotations` in a kube-prometheus-stack values.yaml
renders nothing onto the Deployment — Helm accepts the key and discards it.
The symptom is that downstream controllers (e.g. a stakater/reloader
annotation) never trigger.

`helm template <release> <chart> -f values.yaml | grep -B 2 -A 5
deploymentAnnotations` would have caught this in seconds.
